channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A woman witnessed a heroic act while suffering in an emergency room
item.description: On this week's "My Unsung Hero" from Hidden Brain, one woman says she witnessed a heroic act while suffering in an emergency room.
item.content:encoded: On this week's "My Unsung Hero" from Hidden Brain, one woman says she witnessed a heroic act while suffering in an emergency room.

item.title: Wheelchair curler Steve Emt's path from drunk driver to three-time Paralympian
item.description: Steve Emt and Laura Dwyer represent the U.S. in the Paralympics' new mixed doubles wheelchair curling event. They could bring home Team USA's first wheelchair curling medal ever.
item.content:encoded:
Steve Emt and Laura Dwyer represent the U.S. in the Paralympics' new mixed doubles wheelchair curling event. They could bring home Team USA's first wheelchair curling medal ever.

(Image credit: Maja Hitij)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: 'American Classic' is a hidden gem that gets even better as it goes
item.description: In this charming TV series, Kevin Kline plays a Shakespearean actor who retreats to his small hometown after a crisis, and gets engaged in an effort to save the local theater.
item.content:encoded:
In this charming TV series, Kevin Kline plays a Shakespearean actor who retreats to his small hometown after a crisis, and gets engaged in an effort to save the local theater.

(Image credit: David Giesbrecht)
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: 'My family is enough': Jamilah Lemieux on being a 'Black. Single. Mother.'
item.description: As a culture critic, Lemieux has spent years pushing back against the stereotypes and stigma that follow single mothers. Her new book blends her own memoir with the stories of 21 other Black women.
item.content:encoded: As a culture critic, Lemieux has spent years pushing back against the stereotypes and stigma that follow single mothers. Her new book blends her own memoir with the stories of 21 other Black women.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Out of work and with 2 teens, this mom may lose food stamps under Trump's changes
item.description: Policy experts say new SNAP changes don't address the challenges faced by single parents. They also argue that losing food assistance will only create more barriers for struggling families.
item.content:encoded:
Policy experts say new SNAP changes don't address the challenges faced by single parents. They also argue that losing food assistance will only create more barriers for struggling families.

(Image credit: Caroline Yang for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: This historian dug up the hidden history of 'amateur' blackface in America
item.description: In her new book, Darkology, historian Rhae Lynn Barnes writes about how blackface and minstrel shows became one of the most popular forms of entertainment in 19th- and 20th-century America.
item.content:encoded: In her new book, Darkology, historian Rhae Lynn Barnes writes about how blackface and minstrel shows became one of the most popular forms of entertainment in 19th- and 20th-century America.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Country Joe McDonald, anti-war singer who electrified Woodstock, dies at 84
item.description: Country Joe and the Fish's best-known song, "I-Feel-Like-I'm-Fixin'-to-Die Rag," captured the growing anti-war sentiment of the Vietnam era.
item.content:encoded:
Country Joe and the Fish's best-known song, "I-Feel-Like-I'm-Fixin'-to-Die Rag," captured the growing anti-war sentiment of the Vietnam era.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: Photos: These bold women stand up for justice, rights ... and freedom
item.description: To mark International Women's Day, we feature portraits and profiles of determined women around the world.
item.content:encoded:
To mark International Women's Day, we feature portraits and profiles of determined women around the world.

(Image credit: Annice Lyn)
item.dc:creator: Kamala Thiagarajan

item.title: A grant helps preserve Black churches. We spoke to one of this year's recipients
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe asks Sean Jarrett, pastor of New Jerusalem Baptist Church in Tulsa, Okla., about receiving a grant from the Preserving Black Churches program.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe asks Sean Jarrett, pastor of New Jerusalem Baptist Church in Tulsa, Okla., about receiving a grant from the Preserving Black Churches program.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: Hush
item.description: Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with BPR listener Wesley Davis and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with BPR listener Wesley Davis and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz
